content:
It isn’t because AI can’t write. It’s because writing and saying something aren’t the same thing.

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Matúš Gocman on UnsplashThe first time I read an article that was obviously written by AI, I couldn’t immediately explain why it felt off. The grammar was clean. The structure made sense. Every paragraph flowed neatly into the next.
Nothing was technically wrong with it, yet I forgot almost everything a few minutes later.
At first, I assumed the problem was the wording. Maybe it sounded too polished. Maybe the transitions were too smooth. Maybe the vocabulary felt unnatural.
The more examples I read, though, the less convincing those explanations became.
Some AI-generated articles are surprisingly readable. Some even imitate human style well enough that most readers can’t immediately tell the difference. They use contractions, tell stories, and sprinkle in emotional language.
Yet many of them still leave the same strange feeling behind.
It’s a little like talking to someone who never says anything inaccurate but never reveals anything personal either. The conversation moves forward without resistance.
By the time it ends, you realize nothing actually stayed with you.
